title: Police searching for tandem moped riders after shots fired in Roxbury
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston police are searching for two suspects involved in a shots-fired incident on June 30 in Roxbury.
The suspects, seen wearing black masks, were riding a moped in tandem down Schuyler Street toward Elm Hill Avenue.
Boston Police Department urges anyone with information to contact detectives at (617) 343-4275.

title: Jaylen Brown comforts 6-year-old fan whose reaction to trade went viral
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown comforts 6-year-old fan Giovanni Jean after viral reaction to his trade to the 76ers.
Brown met Jean at his MIT educational program, where Jean handed him a heartfelt letter.
Jean's mother expressed gratitude for the meeting, highlighting her son's admiration for Brown.

title: Fatal Cambridge shooting renews criticism of council vote to end ShotSpotter contract
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Xavier Bautista, 32, was shot and killed in Cambridge early on July 4; his body was discovered an hour later.
The shooting has sparked debate on Cambridge's decision to end its ShotSpotter contract, which some argue could have aided faster response.
Cambridge held a community meeting following the incident; a GoFundMe for Bautista's family raised nearly $14,000.

title: Jaylen Brown on Celtics trade: Boston is home and no organization can take that away
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown continues his community work in Boston despite being traded to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Governor Maura Healey declared July 7 as 7uice Foundation Day in Massachusetts honoring Brown's impact.
Jaylen Brown expressed gratitude to Boston, calling it home, and plans to maintain ties despite the trade.

title: Wrong-way I-95 driver faces drunk driving charges after crash, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: John Tarshi, 25, from Lawrence, charged with drunk driving after wrong-way crash on I-95 in Woburn, no injuries reported.
State Police found forged New Hampshire RMV document and mismatched license plate in Tarshi's vehicle during arrest.
Governor Healey has launched initiative to reduce wrong-way driving following recent incidents, including crash that killed Trooper.

title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming now (July 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jennifer Garner stars in 'The Five-Star Weekend', debuting on Peacock July 9.
A24's 'The Drama' premieres on HBO Max July 31, featuring a Boston filming location.
Prime Video launches Ryan Gosling's sci-fi 'Project Hail Mary' on July 3.

title: Noah Kahan’s historic 4-show Fenway Park run kicks off Tuesday. Here’s everything you need to know.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan begins a historic four-show run at Fenway Park in Boston, starting Tuesday.
Concert nights at Fenway will feature performances by Annabelle Dinda at 6:10 p.m., Gigi Perez at 6:50 p.m., and Noah Kahan at 8 p.m.
Local businesses are offering special promotions and themed items in celebration of Kahan's Fenway shows.

title: Labouré College cuts 65 jobs ahead of Curry College transition
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Labouré College to lay off 65 employees as it closes its Milton campus after Curry College acquisition.
Labouré students will complete degrees at Curry College; nursing programs to transfer with a new advancement center.
Curry to hire 15 nursing faculty and 20 staff from Labouré; unclear if they are among the 65 laid off.

title: Celtics sign Ron Harper Jr. to multi-year contract
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics sign Ron Harper Jr. to a four-year deal worth up to $13.7 million, reports Spotrac.
Harper Jr. averaged 4.2 points and 1.7 rebounds in 29 games this season, starting three games.
Harper Jr. earned third-team All-NBA G-League honors, averaging 25.4 points with the Maine Celtics.

title: ‘Amateur hour’: Mass. sheriff accuses ICE of trying to bully him into breaking the law
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Bristol County Sheriff Paul Heroux accuses ICE of trying to intimidate him into violating state and federal law.
ICE criticized Bristol County for releasing Jose Raul Martinez-Alvarado, a Honduran man with pending gun charges, without honoring a detainer.
Massachusetts law prevents local agencies from complying with ICE detainers unless part of a 287(g) agreement, which Bristol County lacks.
